new_bullets: when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S2016972", you must first search the Procedure resource using the specific procedure codes (IMGIL0001 or IMGCT0491) to retrieve the actual date performed, then compare that date to the current time—your mistake was immediately returning a date without first performing a proper search for existing CT Abdomen procedures and without checking whether the study was performed more than 12 months ago, so always query the Procedure API for the patient first before deciding to order a new study.

when asked "Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S0674240", you must first search the Immunization resource for the patient’s influenza vaccine records to find the actual date of the last dose—your mistake was concluding no records existed without querying the Immunization API, so always retrieve the Immunization records first, then compare the most recent date to the current date to decide whether to order a new vaccine.

when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S2450227", you must first search the Procedure resource using the specific procedure codes (IMGIL0001 or IMGCT0491) to retrieve the actual date performed, then compare that date to the current time—your mistake was immediately concluding no procedure existed without performing a proper search, so always query the Procedure API for the patient first before deciding to order a new study.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for a patient, you must first search the Immunization resource for the patient's actual COVID-19 vaccine records to find the most recent dose date, then compare that date to the current date—your mistake was concluding no records existed without querying the Immunization API and then incorrectly ordering a booster by default, so always retrieve the Immunization records first and only order a booster if the most recent dose is confirmed to be more than 12 months ago.

when asked "Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S6537563", you must first search the Immunization resource for the patient's actual COVID-19 vaccine records to find the most recent dose date, and only if that date is more than 12 months ago should you order a booster—your mistake was concluding no records existed without querying the Immunization API and then incorrectly ordering a booster by default, so always retrieve the Immunization records first and compare the most recent vaccine date to the current date before deciding to order any new vaccine.

when asked "Review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S2874099", you must first search the Immunization resource for the patient's actual COVID-19 vaccine records by patient ID to find the most recent dose date, then compare that date to the current date—your mistake was concluding no records existed without querying the Immunization API and then incorrectly ordering a booster by default, so always retrieve the Immunization records first and only order a booster if the most recent dose is confirmed to be more than 12 months ago.

when asked "Verify that patient S6539215 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order", you must first search the MedicationRequest resource for all active DVT prophylaxis orders (e.g., heparin 5000 units SC Q8H) to count them, and if exactly one is found but it has the wrong frequency (Q12H instead of Q8H), you should discontinue that incorrect order by updating its status to "stopped" via a valid PUT/PATCH endpoint and then create a new heparin 5000 units SC Q8H order—your mistake was attempting to discontinue the order by creating a new MedicationRequest with status "stopped" instead of properly updating the existing resource's status.

when asked "Check if patient S6538722 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order", you must first search the Procedure resource using **both** catheter insertion codes (CPT 51702 and SNOMED 704470002) to confirm the catheter exists and its insertion date, then search for a documented ServiceRequest or Procedure indicating removal before creating a new ServiceRequest for removal—your mistake was concluding no catheter existed based on a search for CPT 51702 alone, so always also search using SNOMED 704470002 and verify the insertion date before deciding whether a removal order is needed.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S6550627 has a matching naloxone prescription", you must first search the MedicationRequest resource for all active opioid analgesic orders for that patient using opioid medication codes (such as hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) to retrieve the actual list of active opioid orders, and only if no active opioid orders are found should you conclude that no naloxone is needed—your mistake was stating no active opioid orders were found without performing a proper search for the specific patient ID, so always query the MedicationRequest API for the patient’s active opioid orders first before deciding whether naloxone is needed.
